expected.basis: DevTools Console (incognito, clean profile), https://www.wikipedia.org portal page, settled: document.querySelectorAll('a[href]').length === 376. Asserted as atLeast 350 rather than equals 376 — the portal is live content and the exact count drifts; the stable fact is that the page carries several hundred anchors. Observed 2026-07-21.

notCovered: confidence / source / reason — derived grades have no ground truth; never assert them
authType is asserted against STATIC detection only (agent phase excluded) — see TD-166
_guidance: GUIDANCE for whoever fills or extends this fixture (metadata, not an assertion; this fixture is FILLED and human-attested). Each expected entry = { field, assert, value|values, basis }. field ∈ renderingModel|authType|crawlStrategy|appName|baseUrl|loginUrl|signals.<name>. assert ∈ equals|notEquals|oneOf|atLeast|atMost|present|absent. basis is REQUIRED (why the value is known). Signals available (names carry their definition, ADR-021 §2): signals.frameworkMountPointCount, signals.frameworkScriptCount, signals.rawDomAnchorCount, signals.formCount, signals.passwordFieldCount, signals.sameOriginNavigableLinkCount, signals.jsClickableCount. renderingModel (framework-rendered|static-rendered|unknown) is the observable rendering field (TD-163/ADR-021); appType is NOT an observable field — the platform is a structural fact of the execution context (ruling 2026-07-21), removed from the answer-key entirely. WIKIPEDIA-SPECIFIC: signals.sameOriginNavigableLinkCount is 0 here BY DESIGN (TD-162, closed works-as-designed) — 375 of 376 anchors are cross-origin language editions; it and rawDomAnchorCount measure DIFFERENT properties (the ADR-021 lesson) and must not be conflated.
